name: Pumpkin Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Pumpkin puree, or other squash
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the crumbs, granulated sugar, and melted butter in a bowl. Press the mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan and about one-third up the sides.
2. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 10 minutes. Remove from the oven and let cool completely.
3. In another bowl, beat the cream cheese until smooth. Gradually add the granulated sugar and vanilla extract, mixing well after each addition. Add the eggs one at a time, stirring well after each addition.
4. Stir in the pumpkin puree and spices (cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves). Mix until just combined.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared crust. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 45-50 minutes, or until the center is set. Let cool on a rack for 15 minutes.
6. Preheat the oven to 475 degrees Fahrenheit. In a separate bowl, mix together the yogurt, remaining granulated sugar, and vanilla extract until combined.
7. Pour evenly over the warm cheesecake. Bake for an additional 10 minutes.
8. Allow the cake to cool completely at room temperature before refrigerating. Refrigerate for at least 12 hours before serving.
Variation: Pumpkin Marble Cheesecake
1. Mix in only half of the 3/4 cup granulated sugar into the cream cheese mixture and do not add pumpkin puree and spices.
2. Instead, mix one cup of the cream cheese mixture with the pumpkin puree and spices. Pour this batter into the crust first.
3. Pour the plain batter (without pumpkin) over the pumpkin batter. Use a knife to swirl the two together.
4. Omit the yogurt topping. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 55 minutes to an hour, or until set.
